input.title: Sports League Management
input.description: Sports League Management is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: League and Season Creation
requirements: The system must support league and season creation as a complete business workflow within the application.
League and Season Creation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with league and season creation from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to league and season creation for operational support.

FR-02.title: Team Registration
requirements: Users must be able to complete team registration through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing team registration to continue.
Team Registration must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for team registration should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-01.title: Plan League and Season Creation Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for league and season creation and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: The system must support league and season creation as a complete business workflow within the application; League and Season Creation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes; Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with league and season creation from the relevant module.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for league and season creation is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for league and season creation are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for league and season creation can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement League and Season Creation Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for league and season creation on React, Node.js, PostgreSQL. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: The system must support league and season creation as a complete business workflow within the application; League and Season Creation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes; Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with league and season creation from the relevant module.
acceptance_criteria: Core league and season creation business rules are implemented for Sports League Management
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for league and season creation
API/service behavior for league and season creation is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement League and Season Creation Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for league and season creation with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The league and season creation user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for league and season creation behave correctly
The league and season creation UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for League and Season Creation
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by league and season creation, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by league and season creation are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for league and season creation integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for league and season creation are handled securely

FR-01.title: Harden League and Season Creation Security and Access Rules
FR-01.description: Apply security controls to league and season creation. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for league and season creation are enforced consistently across API and UI layers
Sensitive operations and data paths in league and season creation are audited and protected against misuse
Security validation for league and season creation passes review and automated checks

FR-01.title: Write League and Season Creation Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for league and season creation. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for league and season creation are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for league and season creation are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical league and season creation scenarios
